title: A necessary condition, and a concrete failure of it
body: We propose resistance to presentation-only review gaming as a necessary condition for AI review automation. Adversarial repackaging breaks it: 75.1% attack success, +1.21 mean score gain, across three models and templates.

title: Structural deficiencies in AI review
body: A strength–weakness asymmetry (easier to impress than to convince) and a strategy effectiveness gradient where narrative reframing far outperforms surface polishing. Systematic, not random.

title: A contamination-free, rolling dataset
body: 500+ recent unpublished arXiv preprints, paired LaTeX + PDF, multi-stage filtered: a benchmark that mimics a real AI-assisted peer-review workflow and can be refreshed as models evolve.

title: A reusable attack framework
body: Full-paper presentation editing + signal-driven strategy selection + closed-loop optimization on the reviewer's own feedback. With the dataset, a reusable benchmark for AI-review robustness.
